strengths: Direct TypeScript/Node.js and React fullstack experience across multiple senior roles, matching the core stack required
Demonstrated platform-building expertise with CRM, automation, and multi-data-source integrations (Optilyz pricing platform, GISCollective)
Hands-on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP work at re:cinq aligns with the nice-to-have LLM/API integration qualification
concerns: Resume emphasizes MongoDB rather than PostgreSQL, though SQL and MySQL are listed
Nest.js is not explicitly mentioned, though deep Node.js experience transfers readily
German listed as basic, which may matter for an on-site German renewable-energy consultancy

key_factors.factor: Platform and integration experience (CRM, automation, geo-data)
AI/LLM and MCP exposure matching nice-to-haves

response.justification: The candidate brings exactly the fullstack TypeScript, React, and Node.js foundation the role requires, proven across pricing platforms, fintech, and a self-built geo-data platform deployable on Kubernetes — directly relevant to a renovation/energy consulting platform that integrates CRM, document, and automation sources. Their software-craftsmanship orientation (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, clean architecture talks and workshops) maps cleanly to the role's emphasis on clean architecture and learning ability, while recent AI-agent and MCP work at re:cinq is a strong bonus against the LLM nice-to-haves. Although the title says junior/mid, the depth here signals a candidate who can take progressively more responsibility from day one.
